original_problem: A factory aims to produce a final batch of exactly 1000 perfectly functional widgets. In their initial production run, 10% of the widgets are found to be defective. These defective widgets are then sent to a re-work department. The re-work department has its own success rate: 80% of the widgets sent for re-work become perfectly functional, while the remaining 20% are deemed irreparable and are scrapped. Assuming all initially defective widgets are sent to re-work, what is the minimum number of widgets the factory must initially produce to guarantee at least 1000 perfectly functional widgets after considering both initial good production and successful re-works?
deconstructed_problem: A factory aims to produce a final batch of exactly 1000 perfectly functional widgets. In their initial production run, 10% of the widgets are found to be defective. These defective widgets are ତାହେଲେ (then) sent to a re-work department. The re-work department has its own success rate: 80% of the widgets sent for re-work become perfectly functional, while the remaining 20% are deemed irreparable and are scrapped. Assuming all initially defective widgets are sent to re-work, what is the minimum number of widgets the factory must initially produce to guarantee at least 1000 perfectly functional widgets after considering both initial good production and successful re-works?
original_steps: Step 1: Identify the two sources of perfectly functional widgets. They come from the initial production that passes inspection and from the defective widgets that are successfully re-worked.

Step 2: Calculate the effective percentage of good widgets from each source relative to the total initial production. If 'X' is the total initial production: 90% of X (0.90X) are good from initial production. The remaining 10% of X (0.10X) are defective and sent to re-work. Of these, 80% become good, so 0.80 * (0.10X) = 0.08X widgets become good from re-work.

Step 3: Determine the total effective percentage of perfectly functional widgets produced from the initial batch. This is the sum of good widgets from initial production and good widgets from re-work: 0.90X + 0.08X = 0.98X. This means 98% of the initial production ultimately results in perfectly functional widgets.
Step 4: Set up an equation to find the required initial production 'X' to meet the target of 1000 perfectly functional widgets. The equation is 0.98X = 1000. Solving for X, X = 1000 / 0.98.

Step 5: Calculate the value of X and round up to the nearest whole number. X ≈ 1020.40816. Since widgets cannot be produced in fractions and the target is 'at least' 1000, the initial production must be rounded up to the next whole widget.
skeleton_steps: ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 1: Identify the two sources of perfectly functional widgets. They come from the initial production that passes inspection and from the defective widgets that are successfully re-worked.

ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 2: Calculate the effective percentage of good widgets from each source relative to the ମୋଟ (total) initial production. ଯଦି (if) 'X' is the ମୋଟ (total) initial production: 90% of X (0.90X) are good from initial production. The remaining 10% of X (0.10X) are defective and sent to re-work. Of these, 80% become good, so 0.80 * (0.10X) = 0.08X widgets become good from re-work.

ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 3: Determine the ମୋଟ (total) effective percentage of perfectly functional widgets produced from the initial batch. This is the ସମଷ୍ଟି (sum) of good widgets from initial production and good widgets from re-work: 0.90X + 0.08X = 0.98X. This means 98% of the initial production ultimately results in perfectly functional widgets.

ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 4: Set up an ସମୀକରଣ (equation) to find the required initial production 'X' to meet the target of 1000 perfectly functional widgets. The ସମୀକରଣ (equation) is 0.98X = 1000. Solving for X, X = 1000 / 0.98.

ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 5: Calculate the value of X and round up to the nearest whole number. X ≈ 1020.40816. Since widgets cannot be produced in fractions and the target is 'at least' 1000, the initial production must be rounded up to the next whole widget.
